subject: Anthropology
title: The Agricultural Revolution
description: Learn The Agricultural Revolution in one minute.

hook.text: For 200,000 years humans hunted and gathered. Then 12,000 years ago, someone planted a seed on purpose.

buildup.text: The Neolithic revolution saw the independent invention of farming in at least seven regions worldwide.

discovery.text: Agriculture allowed food surplus, which enabled permanent settlement, population growth, and social hierarchy.

twist.text: Early farmers were shorter, sicker, and worked longer hours than foragers. Agriculture wasn't obviously better.

climax.text: Farming changed everything: property, inequality, states, and warfare all emerged from the shift to agriculture.

punchline.text: One seed planted the roots of civilization.

quiz.question: What was a consequence of the agricultural revolution?
options: Food surplus enabled hierarchy and permanent settlement
